task_completion.description: Open the 'Smart Home' project in ReqView.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ReqView application is open with the 'Smart Home' project loaded. Look for the project name in the title bar, project tree, or recent files list.
The 'SRS' document is selected in the project tree and its contents are visible in the main document view.

task_completion.description: Identify a requirement containing an IPv4 address in its description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A requirement is selected or visible on screen that clearly contains an IPv4 address (e.g., 192.168.x.x, 10.x.x.x) within its description text.

task_completion.description: Locate the 'DataClassification' attribute for the requirement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'DataClassification' custom attribute is visible in the attributes pane, properties panel, or as a column in the requirements table.
The value 'Confidential' is entered and visible in the 'DataClassification' field for the requirement(s) containing the IPv4 address.
The agent clicks File -> Save, clicks a Save disk icon, presses Ctrl+S, or the UI indicates the project is saved (e.g., an unsaved changes asterisk disappears from the project/document tab).

integrity.description: The agent used the ReqView GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The ReqView application window is actively used to view and edit requirements. The agent does not use terminal commands to modify the project files directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual application interface to change attributes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Modifications to 'DataClassification' are done via the ReqView UI (properties pane or table view), not by opening the SRS.json file in a text editor like nano, vim, or VS Code.

integrity.description: The agent did not use scripts to bypass manual GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are shown executing scripts (e.g., Python, bash, sed, jq) to parse and modify the JSON files automatically.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target attribute to modify is 'DataClassification' and the expected value is 'Confidential'. The SRS document is located at documents/SRS.json within the project. The agent must visually identify requirements containing an IPv4 address in their description text and update their DataClassification attribute to Confidential via the ReqView GUI.
